steps.title: Build line j from two points
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y=6x+4$, so its slope is $6$. Since line $j$ is parallel to $k$, line $j$ also has slope $6$. The point $(0,13)$ is on line $j$; moving $1$ unit right raises $y$ by $6$, so a second point is $(1,19)$.

steps.title: Find line j from two points
steps.content: Use those two points directly. From $(0,13)$ to $(1,19)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{19-13}{1-0}=6.$$ Substituting $(0,13)$ into $y=6x+b$ gives $b=13$, so line $j$ is $y=6x+13$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y=13x+4$: The slope is $13$, not $6$.
  • Choice B — $y=-13x+4$: The slope is $-13$, not $6$.
  • Choice C — $y=6x+13$: The slope is $6$, and the $y$-intercept is $13$.
  • Choice D — $y=-6x+13$: The slope is $-6$, not $6$.
C is correct.
